text: The name John is not mentioned in the original, either here or in verses 6 and 7 below. But it is plain that either John or Judas is meant, and 13.54 seems to give the best grounds for a decision.

v. 9. It is grammatically impossible to make Cendebaeus the subject of the verb ‘he had fortified’, as the sentence stands. It seems likely that there is some slight error or omission in the manuscripts.

‘Of a high priest’; Josephus understands this as referring to Simon himself. But it does not seem likely that the author should have suppressed his name in verse 12, only to mention it in verse 13; nor does he mention the circumstances of affinity as adding to the heinousness of the crime (cf. verse 17). Possibly some other name has dropped out, e.g. that of Alcimus.
